tasks.description: Fix full-width tabs: Edit TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS in src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx (around line 35-38) to add entries for 2 and 5 columns: `2: 'grid-cols-2'` and `5: 'grid-cols-5'`. This ensures effect-layer clips (2 tabs) and any future 5-tab layouts stretch evenly across the panel width.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` contains keys 2, 3, 4, and 5, and `TabsList` still applies `grid w-full` with the selected column class. This means a 2-tab effect-layer clip resolves to `grid-cols-2` and will distribute tabs evenly across the available width. `git show HEAD` matches the same state, so no additional persistent diff was required.

tasks.description: Restore committed HEAD versions of 6 drop-zone files. First, diff each file against HEAD to confirm the unstaged changes are pure reverts of the top-drop-zone feature (not new work). Then run `git checkout HEAD -- <file>` for: coordinate-utils.ts, drop-position.ts, useExternalDrop.ts, useAssetManagement.ts, DropIndicator.tsx, TimelineCanvas.tsx. Do NOT restore ClipPanel.tsx (T1 edits that file independently).
tasks.executor_notes: Verified each unstaged diff before restore. The changes were pure reverts of the top-drop-zone work: `coordinate-utils.ts` reintroduced negative row detection removal, `drop-position.ts` removed `isNewTrackTop` plumbing, `useExternalDrop.ts` and `useAssetManagement.ts` removed `insertAtTop` handling, `DropIndicator.tsx` collapsed the descriptive top/bottom label, and `TimelineCanvas.tsx` removed the top-zone label row. After restoring the `HEAD` content, `git diff --name-only -- <6 files>` and `git status --short -- <6 files>` both returned empty output, confirming all six files now match committed `HEAD`.

tasks.description: Fix indicator plumbing — forward isNewTrackTop AND trackId through the indicator path. Three edits: (1) In DropIndicator.tsx, add `isNewTrackTop?: boolean` and `trackId?: string` to the `DropIndicatorPosition` interface. (2) In useDragCoordinator.ts's `toIndicatorPosition()` function (around line 64-82), add `isNewTrackTop: position.isNewTrackTop` and `trackId: position.trackId` to the returned object. (3) Verify DropIndicator's label generation reads `position?.isNewTrackTop` correctly (it should from the committed HEAD).
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `DropIndicatorPosition` now declares both `isNewTrackTop?: boolean` and `trackId?: string` in `DropIndicator.tsx`. Verified `toIndicatorPosition()` in `useDragCoordinator.ts` forwards both fields from `DropPosition`. Verified the label generation still reads `position?.isNewTrackTop` and updated the effect dependency list to include that field so the top/bottom label stays correct while dragging.

tasks.description: Fix top indicator positioning in TimelineCanvas.tsx. Change the top indicator div from normal document flow to absolute positioning: add `absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 z-10` classes and ensure `pointer-events-none` is present. The parent container (around line 506) is already `position: relative`. This prevents the indicator from pushing track rows down and breaking hit-test coordinate math.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the top indicator div in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now uses `absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 z-10` and retains `pointer-events-none`. Verified its parent container remains `className="relative"`, so the label will overlay the timeline instead of participating in normal flow and shifting row hit-test geometry.

tasks.description: Extend internal clip drag to support top insertion. Three edits: (1) In useTimelineTrackManagement.ts, update `createTrackAndMoveClip` (around line 145) to accept an optional `insertAtTop?: boolean` parameter. When true, pass `0` as the track index to `addTrack()` (which already accepts an optional index param); otherwise pass `undefined` to append. (2) In useClipDrag.ts (around line 370), pass `dropPosition.isNewTrackTop` as the 4th argument to `createTrackAndMoveClipRef.current()`. (3) Update any type declarations for the createTrackAndMoveClip ref/callback to include the new parameter.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified `createTrackAndMoveClip` now accepts `insertAtTop?: boolean` in both the hook result type and callback implementation. When `insertAtTop` is true it now calls `addTrack(current.resolvedConfig, kind, 0)`. Verified `useClipDrag.ts` forwards `dropPosition.isNewTrackTop` as the 4th argument at the single-clip cross-track drop site. Also verified `addTrack()` in `editor-utils.ts` already supports indexed insertion via `splice`, so index `0` insertion is valid.

tasks.description: TypeScript compilation check: run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify no type errors were introduced. Pay special attention to DropIndicatorPosition usages, createTrackAndMoveClip call sites, and TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS lookups.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran `npx tsc --noEmit` from the repository root and it exited successfully with zero output and exit code 0. No TypeScript mismatches were reported at `DropIndicatorPosition` usage sites, `createTrackAndMoveClip` call sites, or the `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` lookup in `ClipPanel.tsx`.

watch_items: [DECISION-003] Top indicator MUST be absolutely positioned with pointer-events-none. Normal flow breaks hit-testing by shifting row coordinates.
[DECISION-005] trackId must be added to DropIndicatorPosition AND forwarded in toIndicatorPosition() alongside isNewTrackTop — the plan's Step 3 is incomplete without this.

[FLAG: workspace-restore-destructive] Before restoring files in T2, diff each unstaged file against HEAD to confirm changes are pure reverts of the top-drop feature, not unrelated new work. If any file has non-revert changes, preserve those manually.
[FLAG: timeline-indicator-typing-trackid] DropIndicator reads position.trackId but the interface doesn't declare it. Must fix in T3 or TS will error / the field will be undefined.
[DEBT: drop-indicator-plumbing] Pre-existing debt item — T3 directly resolves this by forwarding both isNewTrackTop and trackId.
[DEBT: workspace-git-hygiene] Pre-existing debt item — T2 addresses this by diffing before discarding.
[ASSUMPTION] addTrack() in editor-utils.ts correctly handles index=0 (splice at 0) for both tracks and rows arrays. Verify if any downstream logic assumes tracks are only appended.
[ASSUMPTION] createTrackAndMoveClip references new track by ID not index — so inserting at index 0 won't break the clip-move logic that follows track creation.
[REGRESSION] Bottom drop zone must continue working. After all changes, test bottom drops for external files, text tools, effect layers, and internal clip drags.
[REGRESSION] Single-track timeline: both top and bottom zones should work. Empty timeline: drop creates first track (no top zone expected with 0 tracks).

This was verified from code; no browser render was run.

That makes it an overlay rather than a normal-flow element, so it will not push track rows down. This was verified from the rendered class/style code, not via browser interaction.

sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `createTrackAndMoveClip` now accepts `insertAtTop` and passes `0` to `addTrack()` when true, otherwise `undefined`. `useClipDrag` forwards `dropPosition.isNewTrackTop` as the 4th argument. I also verified `addTrack()` already supports indexed insertion with `splice`, so the top-insert path is valid.

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

1. **T1 is fully independent** — do it first as a quick win. The only file it touches (ClipPanel.tsx) is not involved in any other task.

2. **T2 is the critical gate.** The unstaged changes in the working tree revert the top-drop-zone feature that was committed at HEAD. The plan's intent is to discard those reverts and fix forward from the committed code. Before running `git checkout HEAD --`, diff each file to confirm the changes are indeed reverts. The ClipPanel.tsx unstaged change may include the tab fix already — check before restoring that one (it's excluded from T2 for this reason).

3. **T3, T4, T5 are independent of each other** (all depend only on T2) and can be done in parallel or any order. T3 is the most critical — without it, the indicator never shows "at top" and TimelineCanvas never renders the top indicator div.

4. **The trackId gap (DECISION-005)** is not in the original plan text but is called out in gate warnings. The executor MUST add trackId to DropIndicatorPosition and forward it in toIndicatorPosition() as part of T3. Without this, DropIndicator's label generation that reads position?.trackId will get undefined, potentially breaking label text for non-new-track drops.

5. **Coordinate math integrity:** The top indicator being absolutely positioned (T4) is load-bearing for correctness. If it's in normal flow, it adds ~32px of height above the first track row, but computeDropPosition() calculates row positions from editRect.top + scrollTop without that offset — so every row's hit zone shifts down by 32px, breaking all drop targeting. This was one of the 3 verified significant flags.

6. **addTrack's index parameter:** The plan assumes addTrack in editor-utils.ts handles index=0 correctly via splice. Verify this before relying on it in T5. If addTrack only supports append, you'll need to add splice logic.

7. **No automated tests exist for drag-drop behavior.** Validation is manual. The plan's Step 6 checklist covers the test matrix. Key scenarios: external file to top, internal clip to top, bottom zone still works, single-track timeline, empty timeline.
